creator: jin@adv-pc-1.lbl.gov
text:
When doing internal shell (no a login shell), the variable "term"
	supposes to be unset. Somehow, after 2.2.2-RELEASE, the "term" is
	is to "su", which is useless and confusing the conventional ENV.

Fix: 

It should be in /usr/src/libexec/rshd/rshd.c ; but I could not
	find where the "term" has been used. So, a wide guess that the env
	string may be overflowing if no one intends to set it to "su".
How-To-Repeat: 
	any_hosts % rsh FreeBSD env
	HOME=/home/users/jin
	SHELL=/bin/csh
	PATH=/usr/bin:/bin
	USER=jin
	TERM=su				# should not be set at all
	PWD=/home/users/jin
